name: back-face-bleed
_notes: Open the front cover (j=-1 → 0), let it settle, then perform a REVERSE turn (peel the now-open cover back toward the right) and HOLD parked near peak dihedral. Reproduces the pose from Brian's canonical bug capture (contrib/screenshots/anon-2026-05-12T22-19-46-475Z-...png; preserved as contrib/debug/back-face-before.png) where the cream cover_front_int interior was visibly speckled with navy from cover_front_ext bleeding through. Mechanism (PR #36 description): the single DoubleSide turning-page mesh had polygonOffset(-2,-2). At high dihedral the slope-biased offset pulled back-facing fragments forward, breaking the depth test against the front-facing fragments that should have rendered, so the back texture leaked into the front-face area. The fix splits the mesh into FrontSide+BackSide; only FrontSide carries the offset, and the back face renders honestly. Assertion samples the cream cover-interior region near the curl at peak reverse-turn dihedral: with the bug the navy speckles drag mean luma down; with the fix it stays cleanly cream.

assertions.description: Mid-reverse-turn back-of-curl region should render the dark back-face texture (cover_front_ext mirrored, ~navy luma 30-50). Under the back-face bleed bug (PR #31, fixed in PR #36) the slope-biased polygonOffset on the DoubleSide mesh leaked the bright cover_front_int (cream, luma ~210) into the back-facing fragments, raising mean luma above 150. Threshold of 100 cleanly separates the two regimes (post-fix: ~37; pre-fix b22a2c6^: ~203).
